rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.

When AGREE reports type errors like '<error>' or 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace and resolve the root cause of the unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference visible AADL features or helper declarations that are properly typed and resolved.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations to avoid resolution errors.
Place assume/guarantee clauses in appropriate component scopes: type contracts for port envelopes, implementation contracts for output assignments.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes unless matching with explicit 'with' clauses in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must have consistent types on both sides of binary operators like '<='; use eq helpers to ensure correct type matching.
Assign statements in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Constants and equations in AGREE should be declared with explicit types (int, real, bool) to prevent type resolution errors.
anti_patterns: Do not add new property definitions or constants to AGREE annexes unless they are already present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings instead.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
